text.title: Pattern Memory Concepts
text.summary: What a pattern is vs a code snippet, confidence levels, capture triggers, and hierarchical memory architecture.

rule.statement: A pattern must include the problem context, when to apply, and when NOT to apply — not just the code example.
rule.rationale: Agents conflate "pattern" with "code snippet." A pattern applied in the wrong context is worse than no pattern. The problem context is what enables correct routing.

list.item: Pattern: high abstraction (problem + context + solution), cross-project reusability, metadata (validation, provenance), evolves over time
Code snippet: low abstraction (just code), project-specific, no metadata, static
Code patterns: error handling, data management, API design, testing approaches
Architecture patterns: structural (component org, module boundaries), behavioral (event handling, pub-sub), integration (service communication)
Workflow patterns: planning (estimation, decomposition), execution (implementation order, debugging), review (quality gates, code review practices)

rule.statement: Start every new pattern at confidence 0.5; do not promote to patterns.md until at least 2 uses in distinct contexts.
rule.rationale: Confidence scores are self-assessed by agents and inflate over time without external validation. Requiring 2+ distinct-context uses before promotion prevents premature standardization of single-use fixes.

list.item: Initial (0.5): first successful use — capture as candidate
Validated (0.6-0.7): 2-3 successful uses in similar contexts
Established (0.8-0.9): 5+ uses, verified across different contexts — sync to CLAUDE.md
Proven (0.9+): 10+ uses, adopted as standard practice

rule.statement: Capture a pattern when: the solution works in 2+ different contexts, solves a problem seen before, includes a non-obvious insight, or prevents a common mistake.
rule.rationale: Without explicit extraction at task completion, agents never capture patterns. The extraction must be built into the task completion checklist, not left as an optional afterthought.

list.item: Do capture: solutions to recurring problems, non-obvious approaches, cross-context generalizations, mistake preventers
Do not capture: one-off fixes, well-known best practices (already in docs), project-specific configurations, trivial implementations
Working memory (current task): always loaded, session scope
Session memory (current feature): on-demand, conversation scope
Project memory (.aidocs/memory/patterns.md): persistent, queried by relevance
Global memory: permanent, cross-project — used sparingly

rule.statement: Filter patterns.md by relevance to the current task domain before injecting into task context.
rule.rationale: Loading all patterns into every task context wastes tokens. A payment feature task does not need auth patterns injected unless they share a mechanism.
